spec.title: Cinematic Tribute Video for Guru Vandana cum Birthday Event (Shri Anil Tolia Ji, 250 guests)
spec.grounding_note:
REAL (verbatim from the client's fulldesc in db_all.json): an "Emotional Life Journey Tribute Video" for the "Guru Vandana" + Birthday celebration of Shri Anil Tolia Ji, to be "played on a large screen during the family function in front of friends, family members, children, and guests" (~250 people). The 8-section storytelling flow is real and quoted exactly: (1) Opening Tribute, (2) Childhood Journey, (3) College & Friendship Days, (4) Career & Achievements, (5) Family Journey, (6) Guru Vandana Section, (7) Birthday Celebration, (8) Closing Message. Real: warm/mature/respectful voice narration in primarily English with optional Hindi/Gujarati elements (AI narration explicitly allowed); soft piano / light orchestral / flute royalty-free background music that "blends naturally with visuals" and does "not overpower narration"; smooth cinematic transitions, slow zoom/pan, elegant typography; preferred duration 5–8 minutes; Full HD 1920×1080 MP4 "suitable for LED screen/projector playback"; deliverables = final HD MP4 + editable/source files + "music and asset details used" + one or two revision rounds; budget ₹1500–₹12500 INR; tools the client named include Premiere Pro, After Effects, Canva Pro, and AI narration (Synthesia). The "Content Available" list is real: childhood, college, friends/social, professional/work, marriage & family, recent, and Guru-Vandana photos, plus "Additional content may include short messages/quotes, milestones/dates, family inputs."

ENRICHED (only where the brief genuinely implies it, made explicit so it is honestly long-horizon): The brief says the tribute is built "through photographs, music, voice narration, and cinematic storytelling" and lists raw "family inputs" — I enrich the supplied content to include a handful of RAW EVENT/HOME VIDEO CLIPS (a noisy on-stage live tribute speech a family elder gives, plus candid home/career clips) alongside the photos, because a 250-guest live function realistically yields phone video and because the named toolset (Premiere/After Effects/DaVinci) is video-editing software. This makes media_summarize (find emotional highlights across many raw clips), video_create_quick_cut (auto-cut a sizzle from chosen assetIds), and media_enhance_speech (clean the noisy live on-stage speech to reuse as narration) all faithful to the deliverable rather than invented. The "stage-screen master + a short social/WhatsApp share cut" is a light, defensible enrichment: the family explicitly wants the piece for a large screen, and event families routinely also want a vertical clip to share on the family WhatsApp/Instagram afterward — the brief's audience ("friends, family members, children, and guests") supports this. Nothing about the emotional, non-flashy, premium, royalty-free, HD-MP4 contract is changed.
spec.one_line_ask: Assemble a 5–8 minute cinematic, emotionally-paced Full-HD 1920×1080 tribute video for Shri Anil Tolia Ji's Guru Vandana + birthday function — built from the family's raw photos and event clips, following the 8-section life-journey flow, with a cleaned warm narration VO over soft royalty-free piano/orchestral score — and deliver both the stage-screen master and a short vertical social share cut plus an asset-credits sheet.
spec.full_brief:
Context: A family is organizing a "Guru Vandana" (guru-reverence) cum birthday celebration for Shri Anil Tolia Ji, to be attended by roughly 250 friends, family members, children, and guests. The centerpiece of the function is an emotional life-journey tribute video projected on a large LED screen/projector during the event. The piece must feel emotional but positive, respectful and heartwarming, inspirational, elegant and premium — and must AVOID loud flashy editing, overly dramatic effects, fast social-media-style cutting, and distracting animations. The desired register is "cinematic family tribute / elegant slideshow-documentary," with smooth transitions, slow zoom/pan, and tasteful typography.

The ask: Using the supplied raw materials — chronological photo sets (childhood, college & friendship, career, marriage & family, recent, and Guru-Vandana moments), several raw home/event video clips, and a noisy live on-stage tribute speech recorded at rehearsal — produce a 5–8 minute Full-HD (1920×1080, MP4) tribute that follows the client's exact 8-section flow: (1) Opening Tribute, (2) Childhood Journey, (3) College & Friendship Days, (4) Career & Achievements, (5) Family Journey, (6) Guru Vandana Section, (7) Birthday Celebration, (8) Closing Message. The agent must mine the raw clips for the most emotional, usable moments, auto-cut a tight cinematic "memory sizzle" from the chosen clip segments to seed the montage, clean the noisy on-stage tribute speech into a warm, broadcast-clean narration track (or generate an equivalent warm/mature English VO with optional Hindi/Gujarati blessing lines), score the whole thing with soft, licensed, royalty-free piano/light-orchestral/flute music that blends under — and never overpowers — the narration, and lay elegant section titles and a closing thank-you over the cut.

Constraints & deliverables: Output 1 = the stage-screen MASTER at exactly 1920×1080 (16:9), H.264 MP4, 5–8 minutes, clean enough for large-screen projection, music bed mixed roughly 14–18 dB under the VO. Output 2 = a short (≤60s) vertical 1080×1920 (9:16) social/WhatsApp share cut reframed subject-aware from the master's best moments, same audio treatment. Output 3 = an asset-credits / music-and-source sheet (PDF) listing every licensed Stock track and the source clips used, as the client explicitly requires "music and asset details used." All music must be royalty-free/licensed; narration must be warm, encouraging, emotional, mature and respectful; nothing flashy. The family expects one or two revision rounds, so the agent must keep the intermediate assets (cleaned VO, licensed score, the sizzle, the master) individually addressable for re-edits.

inputs.name: childhood_journey_photos
inputs.gen_prompt: A small set (treat as 6–8 frames) of authentic-looking 1960s–70s Indian family snapshots of a baby/young boy named Anil: faded warm-toned film photographs, slightly soft focus, real film grain and dust, period-correct clothing, a modest middle-class Gujarati home, a school class photo on a bench, a boy in shorts holding a slate. Genuine vintage photo-paper texture, real skin with natural imperfections, individual flyaway hairs, slightly off white balance as old prints have. Candid and unposed, documentary, NOT a glossy AI render, no modern artifacts, no text watermark.
inputs.realism_notes: Must read as scanned old prints: uneven exposure, paper grain, a fold crease or two, period color cast. No CGI sheen. Used as the chronological opening of the montage and to seed the section-1/2 mood.

inputs.name: college_friendship_photos
inputs.gen_prompt: Authentic late-1980s Indian college-life snapshots of the same man (Anil) as a young adult: friends grouped on hostel steps, a cricket-team photo, a scooter, a canteen moment. Mild 1980s film grain, warm faded color, real candid expressions, natural skin pores and stubble, individual hair strands and flyaways, correct hands. Documentary, slightly imperfect framing as friends' cameras would shoot. NOT glossy, no modern phones, no text overlay.
inputs.realism_notes: Period clothing/hairstyles and analog color shift sell the 'college & friendship days' chapter. Candid, not staged-perfect.

inputs.name: career_achievement_photos
inputs.gen_prompt: Professional/work-life photos of the same man across his 40s–50s: at an office desk, receiving a small award/momento on a stage, shaking hands at a business event, a factory or shop-floor visit. Realistic 2000s–2010s digital-camera look, natural indoor lighting with slight flash, real skin texture with pores and faint wrinkles, correct hands and fingers, lanyard/spectacles. Candid documentary, not a stock-photo smile, no text or logos baked in.
inputs.realism_notes: Should look like genuine event/work photos across two decades. Reused for the 'career & achievements' chapter and again, color-graded, in the closing recap.

inputs.name: marriage_family_photos
inputs.gen_prompt: Warm Indian marriage and family photographs of the same man: a 1990s wedding portrait in traditional Gujarati attire with garlands, later family photos with wife and growing children, a festival gathering, grandchildren in recent years. Mix of slightly-faded 90s prints and crisp recent digital frames. Real skin, individual hair strands, genuine candid emotion (a tear, a laugh), correct hands, authentic jewelry and clothing. Documentary family-album feel, NOT CGI-glossy, no watermark.
inputs.realism_notes: Emotional core of the piece. Spans decades so the grade must unify them. Reused across family + closing chapters.

inputs.name: guru_vandana_recent_photos
inputs.gen_prompt: Recent (2020s) photographs themed around guru-reverence and the honoree as an elder/mentor: the man touching an elder guru's feet in respect, garlanding a guru, sitting in a satsang/temple setting, mentoring younger people, a recent crisp portrait in a kurta with a warm dignified expression. Natural daylight and lamp-lit temple tones, real skin with age texture, individual grey hairs, correct hands in pranam gesture. Respectful, documentary, premium but authentic — NOT an AI-perfect render, no text.
inputs.realism_notes: Carries the 'Guru Vandana' chapter and the dignified closing portrait. Recent, sharp, but still candid.

inputs.name: raw_home_event_clips
inputs.gen_prompt: A handful of raw, lightly-shaky handheld phone clips (1080p, native audio) from family life and the run-up to the function: the honoree laughing with grandchildren in a living room; cutting a cake at a small home gathering with off-camera chatter; a slow pan across a decorated stage with marigold garlands before guests arrive; the honoree walking in and folding hands to greet people. Natural ambient room sound, imperfect handheld framing, real people with natural skin/hair, candid documentary look. NOT a polished commercial; phone-video realism with minor motion blur.
inputs.realism_notes: These are the raw clips the agent must SUMMARIZE to find emotional highlights and then QUICK-CUT into a sizzle. Native-audio 1080p (not 720p veo-fast). Several distinct moments so summarization and selection are non-trivial.

inputs.name: onstage_tribute_speech_raw
inputs.gen_prompt: A ~90-second warm spoken tribute in English with a few Hindi/Gujarati blessing phrases, delivered as if by a family elder at a microphone: 'Tonight we honour Anil bhai — a son, a friend, a guide... aaj hum sab unke charno mein...'. Warm, mature, emotional, respectful male voice. Generate clean at 48 kHz, THEN (for this audio-repair task only) the supplied file is degraded to simulate a live PA capture: add room reverb, hum, mic pops, crowd murmur and uneven levels, so the agent has a genuinely noisy on-stage recording to enhance.
inputs.realism_notes: This is the ONE input that is intentionally roughened, because the task literally includes audio-repair: the agent must run media_enhance_speech on it to recover a broadcast-clean narration bed. Content is the real tribute language the family would use.

inputs.name: narration_script_and_milestones
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a JSON file with: (a) the 8 section titles exactly per the client flow (Opening Tribute, Childhood Journey, College & Friendship Days, Career & Achievements, Family Journey, Guru Vandana Section, Birthday Celebration, Closing Message); (b) for each section, an elegant on-screen caption line and an estimated duration in seconds (totalling 5–8 min); (c) key milestones/dates and short quotes the family supplied; (d) a warm 6–8 line closing thank-you message; (e) a music-mood tag per section (soft piano / light orchestral / flute). Mature, respectful, English with two optional Hindi/Gujarati blessing lines.
inputs.realism_notes: Drives the typography, pacing, and chapter structure. Reused when laying titles and when building the credits sheet.

outputs.name: tribute_master_stage_screen.mp4
outputs.spec: 1920×1080 (16:9), H.264 MP4, 5–8 minutes, ~24–30 fps, follows all 8 client sections in order, cleaned warm narration VO over a licensed soft piano/orchestral score mixed ~14–18 dB under the VO, elegant section titles + closing thank-you, smooth slow transitions only (no flashy cuts). Suitable for LED-screen/projector playback at the 250-guest function.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: exact 1920×1080; runtime within 5–8 min; all 8 named sections present and in order; narration is clean/intelligible (no PA hum/crowd from the raw speech survives); music audibly sits UNDER the VO and is the soft/uplifting style requested (not flashy); pacing is slow/cinematic with no fast social-style cutting; titles are tasteful and match the script; emotional, premium, respectful tone.

outputs.name: tribute_social_share_cut.mp4
outputs.spec: 1080×1920 (9:16) vertical, H.264 MP4, ≤60 seconds, subject-aware reframed from the master's strongest moments, same cleaned VO + score, suitable for family WhatsApp/Instagram share.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: exact 1080×1920 vertical; ≤60s; the honoree stays centered/in-frame after reframing (subject-aware, no decapitated heads); audio matches the master's clean mix; it is clearly derived from the master, not a separate edit.

outputs.name: tribute_cinematic_sizzle.mp4
outputs.spec: 1920×1080, H.264 MP4, ~20–40 seconds, the auto-cut highlight reel of the strongest raw-clip moments used to seed the montage (intermediate deliverable kept addressable for revisions).
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: the sizzle is genuinely built from the chosen raw-clip segments (traceable to the summarization picks), is tight and emotional, and is reused inside the master rather than discarded.

outputs.name: narration_clean.wav
outputs.spec: 48 kHz WAV (or AAC), the enhanced/denoised version of the on-stage tribute speech — broadcast-clean, PA hum/reverb/crowd removed, levels normalized.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator A/Bs against onstage_tribute_speech_raw: noise/reverb/crowd reduced, intelligibility and warmth preserved, no robotic artifacts, consistent loudness.

outputs.name: asset_credits_sheet.pdf
outputs.spec: A4/Letter PDF listing every licensed Stock music track (title + Stock asset ID + license), each source clip/photo set used, the narration source, and final render specs — satisfying the client's 'music and asset details used' deliverable.
outputs.rubric_hook: Annotator checks: every licensed track and source asset actually used in the master is listed with its real Stock ID; render specs (1920×1080, runtime) match the delivered master; readable, organized, complete.

connector_workflow.output: CC-storage URNs/assetIds for every supplied raw clip, the speech audio, and all photo sets
connector_workflow.note: Ingest all family-supplied raw media into CC storage so later video/audio tools can reference stable assetIds. Establishes the assetId namespace reused throughout.

connector_workflow.output: per-clip scene/transcript summaries with timestamped emotional-highlight notes for each raw event clip
connector_workflow.note: Summarize each raw clip to locate the most emotional, usable moments (cake-cutting, greeting guests, grandchildren) before any cutting — the agent must read these to choose segments. Pure intelligence step feeding the cut.

connector_workflow.output: transcript + content summary of the noisy on-stage tribute speech (so the agent knows what the narration says and which lines to keep)
connector_workflow.note: Summarize/transcribe the raw speech audio so the narration content and Hindi/Gujarati blessing lines are known before enhancement and before writing on-screen captions.

connector_workflow.output: verified list of the stored photo assetIds grouped per chapter (childhood/college/career/family/guru-recent)
connector_workflow.note: Confirm and organize the chronological photo assetIds in CC so the montage chapters map to the 8-section flow. State-tracking checkpoint: which assetId belongs to which chapter.

connector_workflow.output: tribute_cinematic_sizzle.mp4 — auto-cut ~20–40s highlight reel from the chosen raw-clip assetIds + segments
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. Feed the assetIds of the raw clips plus the highlight choices from step 2's summaries (target_duration ~30s, prompt = warm emotional family-tribute highlights) to auto-cut a cinematic sizzle that seeds the montage. Output→input chain: step 2 picks, step 5 cuts.

connector_workflow.output: narration_clean.wav — denoised, de-reverbed, level-normalized version of the on-stage tribute speech
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. Clean the noisy PA capture into a broadcast-clean warm narration bed. Step 3's transcript confirms intelligibility was preserved. This becomes the primary VO track.

connector_workflow.output: all chapter photos regraded with a single warm cinematic look so the decades-spanning prints feel unified
connector_workflow.note: Apply one cinematic/warm Lightroom-style preset across every photo set so 1960s prints and 2020s digital frames share a premium, non-flashy grade. Unifies the slideshow visually before assembly.

connector_workflow.output: licensed royalty-free soft piano / light-orchestral / flute score track(s) with real Stock asset IDs, downloaded to CC storage
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. License a soft, uplifting, royalty-free music bed matching the per-section mood tags from the script, sized to underlay the cleaned narration. Real Stock IDs captured here are reused in the credits sheet (step 15).

connector_workflow.output: an elegant Express title/lower-third template suitable for the 8 section titles and the closing thank-you
connector_workflow.note: Find a tasteful, premium typography template for the section titles and closing message — 'elegant typography', not flashy. Feeds the title fills in step 10.

connector_workflow.output: 8 filled section-title cards + closing thank-you card (text from the script), layout preserved
connector_workflow.note: Fill the chosen template with the exact 8 section titles and the closing thank-you from narration_script_and_milestones. Output→input: step 9 template + script data → titled cards consumed by the assembly step.

connector_workflow.output: an ordered 1920×1080 visual edit-decision-list / storyboard: graded photos + sizzle + title cards laid into the 8 sections with slow zoom/pan timing per the script durations
connector_workflow.note: Deterministically lay the graded photos, the seed sizzle (step 5), and the title cards (step 10) into the exact 8-section order with per-section durations from the script — the visual spine of the montage before muxing. Reuses nearly every prior visual output.

connector_workflow.output: level/loudness + duration report on the cleaned VO vs. the licensed score, to set the music ~14–18 dB under the VO and confirm total runtime sits in 5–8 min
connector_workflow.note: Re-summarize the cleaned narration and the licensed track to verify durations and relative loudness so the mux mixes music safely under the VO (a real client constraint). State-check before render.

connector_workflow.output: tribute_master_stage_screen.mp4 — 1920×1080 5–8 min H.264 master: rendered storyboard + cleaned VO + score ducked per step 12 + transitions
connector_workflow.note: Unavoidable glue: ffmpeg mux of the visual spine (step 11), the cleaned narration (step 6), and the licensed score ducked to the step-12 levels into the final timed master with slow crossfades. Minimal local step; everything it consumes came from connector outputs.

connector_workflow.output: tribute_social_share_cut.mp4 — 1080×1920 vertical, ≤60s, subject-aware reframe of the master's strongest stretch
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. Subject-aware reframe of the finished 16:9 master to a 9:16 vertical share cut, keeping the honoree centered. Output→input: the step-13 master is the sole source. (Trim to ≤60s as part of the resize/export pass.)

connector_workflow.output: asset_credits_sheet.pdf — the music-and-asset-details deliverable listing every licensed track (Stock IDs from step 8), source assets (step 4), narration source, and final render specs (from the step-13 master)
connector_workflow.note: Build the client-required 'music and asset details used' sheet into a PDF: pulls the real Stock license IDs from step 8, the source photo/clip inventory from step 4, and the delivered master's specs from step 13. Closes the contract.

spec.iterative_chaining_note: The chain is genuinely output→input throughout. (1) asset_add_file mints the assetId namespace every later tool references. (2/3) media_summarize reads the raw clips and the noisy speech; (5) video_create_quick_cut consumes step 2's highlight picks to auto-cut the sizzle; (6) media_enhance_speech consumes the raw speech (and step 3's transcript) to produce narration_clean.wav. (4→7) asset_search organizes the photo assetIds, then image_apply_preset regrades exactly those into a unified look. (8) asset_license_and_download_stock returns real Stock IDs that are consumed twice — by the mix and by the credits PDF. (9→10) search_design's template + the script JSON feed fill_text to make the 8 title cards. (11) document_render_layout pulls together the sizzle (5), graded photos (7), and title cards (10) into the timed 8-section spine. (12) media_summarize re-reads the cleaned VO (6) and licensed score (8) to set ducking/runtime. (13) the local ffmpeg mux consumes the spine (11) + VO (6) + score (8) + levels (12) to render the master. (14) video_resize takes ONLY the step-13 master and reframes it to the vertical cut. (15) document_convert_pdf consumes the Stock IDs (8), the asset inventory (4), and the master's specs (13) to produce the credits sheet. No step invents data it wasn't handed by an earlier step; the master is literally built from the sizzle, the cleaned speech, the licensed score, and the graded photos produced upstream.
spec.difficulty_rationale: This is hard for an agent on every long-horizon axis. (1) Horizon & breadth: 15 ordered connector calls spanning five capability groups (video/audio, stock/storage, photo, Express template, layout/PDF), with the must-exercise video+audio tools all in play. (2) Heavy state tracking: the agent must maintain a stable assetId namespace from step 1 and remember which photo assetId belongs to which of the 8 named chapters, which raw-clip segments the summaries flagged, and which Stock IDs were licensed — then surface those exact Stock IDs again 7 steps later in the credits PDF. Drop a mapping and the montage chapters or the credits sheet break. (3) Asset reuse: nearly every input is consumed in 3–4 different steps (photos: ingest→organize→grade→layout→credits; raw clips: summarize→cut; speech: summarize→enhance→mix), so the agent cannot treat inputs as one-shot. (4) Exact-spec pressure: 1920×1080 master in a strict 5–8 min window, music mixed 14–18 dB under the VO, a separate exact 1080×1920 ≤60s vertical cut, and a credits PDF whose stated specs must match the actually-delivered master. (5) Judgment under a 'no-flashy' aesthetic constraint: the client explicitly forbids fast/dramatic/social-style editing, so the agent must choose slow transitions and tasteful titles, not default to a punchy sizzle look. (6) True chaining: the final master is only correct if the sizzle, the cleaned narration, the licensed score, and the unified grade were all produced and threaded correctly upstream — a single broken link is visible in the deliverable.

issues: MAJOR (feasibility): Step 14 / Output 2 cannot be produced by video_resize as routed. CONNECTOR_CAPABILITIES_v2.md line 68 explicitly states video_resize is 'reframe/resize a video to a target ratio (subject-aware), SAME LEN'. The spec requires turning the 5-8 minute master into a <=60s vertical cut and even admits 'Trim to <=60s as part of the resize/export pass' (step 14 note). video_resize does NOT change duration, so the 9:16 <=60s share cut is infeasible via the single tool the chain assigns to it.

MINOR (tool-capability overstatement): Step 12 uses media_summarize to produce a 'level/loudness + duration report ... to set the music ~14-18 dB under the VO'. The sheet (A5, line 70) defines media_summarize as transcript/scene summary only. It does not return precise dB loudness/level measurements, so the 14-18 dB ducking target cannot be sourced from this step as written.

MINOR (tool-capability overstatement): Step 11 uses document_render_layout to produce a '1920x1080 visual edit-decision-list / storyboard ... with slow zoom/pan timing per the script durations'. The sheet (A2, line 36) describes document_render_layout as an InDesign-class page-layout renderer (JSON layout -> laid-out page, text+images, columns), not a video EDL/timeline or motion-timing engine. Per-section zoom/pan timing is actually realized in the step-13 ffmpeg mux, not by this tool.

WEAK CHAIN LINK (not disqualifying): Step 3 media_summarize (transcript of raw speech) is listed as an input to step 6 media_enhance_speech, but enhance_speech denoises the raw audio directly and does not consume a transcript; the note frames it as verification ('confirms intelligibility preserved'), not a true data dependency. Similarly step 12's output is not a genuine data input to the step-13 mux. Overall chain still has well over 12 genuine output->input links, so calls_genuine still passes.
fixes: Fix the vertical-cut routing: do NOT rely on video_resize to trim. Either (a) insert a trim/selection step before reframing - e.g., run video_create_quick_cut on the step-13 master (or on the sizzle/best stretch) with target_duration <=60s to produce a <=60s 16:9 clip, THEN video_resize that clip to 1080x1920 (preserving its now-<=60s length); or (b) do the <=60s trim in the local ffmpeg step and feed the trimmed clip to video_resize. Update step 14's note to remove 'Trim to <=60s as part of the resize/export pass' since video_resize is same-len.

Re-route the ducking-level determination in step 12: get the VO and music loudness/duration from the local ffmpeg step (e.g., ffmpeg/loudnorm/ebur128 or ffprobe) rather than media_summarize, OR restrict step 12's media_summarize claim to duration/runtime confirmation only and explicitly state the -14 to -18 dB ducking is computed/applied in the step-13 local mux.

Reword step 11 so document_render_layout only renders the static 1920x1080 elements it actually can (section title cards, per-section composited frames/photo layouts at fixed positions), and move all 'slow zoom/pan timing per section durations' language into step 13 (the local ffmpeg mux), which is where motion and timing are genuinely realized.

Optional tightening for honest chaining: reframe step 3's role as feeding the on-screen caption/script content (which it genuinely informs) rather than as an input to media_enhance_speech, and have media_enhance_speech in step 6 consume only the raw audio (step 1).
